learning_outcomes.description: Apply traditional financial valuation methodologies and the concepts of marginal, fixed and total costs directly relating to a resource project.
Determine and justify the optimal mine planning strategy according to the key levers for value creation for stakeholders, given local constraints
Critically reflect on a proposed mining plan using Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Knowledges and Perspectives to determine the sustainability implications for key stakeholders.
Apply modern optimisation algorithms in standard industry software to support the decision-making process for generating the strategic mine plan.
Assess and justify the viability of strategic mining plans using financial technical modelling to determine financial, technical and sustainable trade-offs.
Determine the post-mining environmental impacts to recommend modifications to mining plans for suitable post-mining land use.
Work effectively in teams to communicate on the financial, technical and sustainable outcomes of a mining project.

assessments.task_description:
This is a take home task to be completed individually.

It will comprise of a series of short answer and calculation questions relating to material covered in weeks 1-6. The value of each question will be provided upon release of the item. Further details about this task will be provided closer to its release.

Please refer to Blackboard for detailed marking criteria.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Please prepare a written report that provides the working and answer to each question. Submission of excel files is not appropriate.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This project is to be completed individually.

It will comprise of a series of tasks required in the strategic planning of a mining operation. The value of each task will be provided upon release of the item. Further details about this task will be provided closer to its release. No specialist mine planning software is to be used in this task. Complete working in Excel only.

Please prepare a slide pack that provides the answer to each task. Submission of excel files are also required which shows detailed working within the template file that will be provided. This project will culminate in an individual interview after the submission of the slide pack and excel files at a date to be determined.

Please refer to Blackboard for detailed marking criteria.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via Blackboard by the due date/time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This project will consist of an activity requiring the use of the Whittle 4X Software and Deswik Packages.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.

Please prepare and submit a written report.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
